                                                                                 U.S. DEPARTMENT OF LABOR
                                                                                          Ray Marshall, Secretary

                                                                                     BUREAU OF LABOR STATISTICS
                                                                                       Julius Shiskin, Commissioner



                                                                                Employment and Earnings is prepared by the
                                                                            Division of Industry Employment Statistics and the
                                                                            Division of Employment and Unemployment Anal-
                                                                            ysis in collaboration with the Division of Special
                                                                            Publications. The data are collected by the Bureau
                                                                            of the Census (Department of Commerce), Employ-
                                                                            ment and Training Administration, State Employ-
                                                                            ment Security Agencies, and State Departments of
                                                                            Labor in cooperation with the Bureau of Labor
                                                                            Statistics. A brief description of the cooperative
                                                                            statistical programs of the BLS with these agencies
                                                                            is presented in the Explanatory Notes. The State
                                                                            agencies are listed on the inside back cover.
                                                                                 Employment and Earnings may be ordered
                                                                            through the Superintendent of Documents, U.S.
                                                                            Government Printing Office, Washington, D.C.
                                                                            20402. Subscription price per year $18 domes-
                                                                            tic, and $22.50 foreign. Single copy $1.50 domes-
                                                                            tic, and $1.90 foreign. Prices are subject to change
                                                                            by the U.S. Government Printing Office.

                                                                                Communications on editorial matters should be
                                                                            addressed to: Editors, Employment and Earnings,
                                                                            Bureau of Labor Statistics, Washington, D.C. 20212.
                                                                            Inquiries regarding the text and Household Data
                                                                            should be addressed to: Attention of Gloria P,
                                                                            Green or Phone: (202) 523-1944. Inquiries relating
                                                                            to Establishment Data and all other tables should be
                                                                            addressed to: Attention of Chester L. Green or
                                                                             Phone: (202) 523-1759. Send correspondence on
                                                                            circulation and subscription matters (including ad-
                                                                            dress changes) to the Superintendent of Documents.

                                                                                The Secretary of Labor has determined that
                                                                            the publication of this periodical is necessary in
                                                                            the transaction of the public business required by
                                                                            law of this Department. Use of funds for printing
                                                                            this periodical has been approved by the Director
                                                                            of the Office of Management and Budget through
                                                                            February 28, 1981. Controlled circulation postage
                                                                            paid at Washington, D.C. and Baltimore, Md.

                                Note to Subscribers                         Library of Congress Catalog Number 70-11379

          Effective with this issue, the subscription price of Employment       Unless identified as copyrighted, articles and
          and Earn/ngshas been lowered to $18.00 domestic, and $22.50       tabulations in this publication are in the public
                                                                            domain and may be reproduced without permis-
          foreign. Single copy $1.50 domestic, and $1.90 foreign.
                                                                            sion of the Federal Government. Please credit Em-
                                                                            ployment and Earnings, Bureau of Labor Statistics.

                            Editors' Note

                           Publication of State and area annual averages (usually published in the May issue) has been delayed due
                           to processing problems.

                                         In addition to the monthly data appearing regularly in Employment and
                                         Earnings, special features appear in most of the issues, as shown below

                       The issue that introduces new benchmarks varies. The October 1975 issue marks the introduction of March 1974
                 benchmarks. Interim revisions of selected series appear in the February 1977 issue.

                 MONTHLY UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE DATA

                       F- 1: Insured unemployment under State programs                                              115
                       F- 2: Insured unemployment in 150 major labor areas                                          116




                                       Discontinuance of Tables F-1 and F-2
                              Beginning with the July 1977 issue, insured unemployment data (tables F-1 and F-2)
                          will no longer be published in Employment and Earnings. These data will continue to be
                          published by the U.S. Department of Labor's Employment and Training Administration
                          in Unemployment Insurance Claims and Unemployment Insurance Statistics. Inquiries
                          regarding these publications should be addressed to the Employment and Training Admini-
                          stration, Washington, D.C. 20210.

http://fraser.stlouisfed.org/
Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis

                              Federal economic assistance programs and may be ordered from the National Technical Information Service, U.S. Department of Commerce,
                              5285 Port Royal Road, Springfield, Virginia 22161. When ordering please, specify "CETA Area Employment and Unemployment, January-
                              February 1977 (NTISUB/C/143-77/004), "States and County Employment and Unemployment January-February 1977" (NTISUB/C/
                              144-77/004) or "Unemployment Rates for States and Identifiable Local Governments, Fourth Quarter, 1976" (PB264036). Tabulations are
                              available at $6.75 per set for printed copies of CETA Areas, $10.00 for States and Counties, $5.50 for Identifiable Local Governments, or
                              $3.00 per set for microfiche copies of each publication.

          COLLECTION                                                                        State agencies mail the forms to the establishments and
                                                                                         examine the returns for consistency, accuracy, and complete-
             Payroll reports provide current Information on wage and                     ness. The States use the information to prepare State and area
          salary employment, hours, earnings, and labor turnover in non-                 series and then send the establishment data to the BLS for use
          agricultural establishments, by industry and geographic location.              in preparing the national series.


          Federal-State cooperation                                                      Shuttle schedules

              Under cooperative arrangements with State agencies, the                        Two types of data collection schedules are used: Form BLS
          respondent fills out a single employment or labor turnover                     790—Monthly Report on Employment, Payroll, and Hours; and
          reporting form, which is then used for national, State, and area               Form DL 1219—Monthly Report on Labor Turnover. These
          estimates. This eliminates duplicate reporting on the part of                  schedules are of the "shuttle" type, with space for each month
          respondents, and together with the use of identical techniques                 of the calendar year. The collection agency returns the schedule
          at the national and State levels, insures maximum comparability                to the respondent each month so that the next month s data
          of estimates.                                                                  can be entered. This procedure assures maximum comparability

         Labor turnover
                                                                                 From a sample composed of establishments reporting for
             Labor turnover is the gross movement of wage and salary         both the previous and current months, the ratio of current
        workers into and out of employed status with respect to indi-        month employment to that of the previous month is computed.
        vidual establishments. This movement, which relates to a cal-        This is called a link relative. The estimates of employment (all
        endar month, is divided into two broad types: Accessions (new        employees, including production and nonproduction workers

         initiated by either employer or employee). Each type of action      estimates for the previous month by these "link relatives." In

                                                  Unemployment insurance data
                                                                    (F tables)

           Insured unemployment represents the number of persons             expressed as a percent of average covered employment in a
       reporting a week of unemployment under an unemployment                12-month period ending 6 to 8 months prior to the week of
       insurance program. It includes some persons who are working           reference. Initial claims are notices filed by those losing jobs
       part time who would be counted as employed in the payroll and         covered by an unemployment insurance program that they are
       household surveys. Excluded are persons who have exhausted            starting a period of unemployment. A claimant who continued
       their benefit rights and workers who have not earned rights to        to be unemployed a full week is then counted in the insured
       unemployment insurance. In general, excluded from coverage            unemployment figure.
       are those persons engaged in agriculture, domestic service,
       unpaid family work, selected nonprofit organizations, some                Because of differences in State laws and procedures under
       State and local government and self-employment. Also excluded         which unemployment insurance programs are operated, State
       from the insured unemployment count, but included as em-              unemployment rates generally indicate, but do not precisely
       ployed in the household survey, are those persons who earned          measure, differences among the individual States. Persons
